agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"order_history_service","to":"postgresql_primary","status":"risk","issue":"All traffic (95% reads + 5% writes) hits primary only. Read replicas exist but are unused, creating unnecessary load on primary and wasting provisioned replica capacity."},{"from":"order_history_service","to":"postgresql_primary","status":"broken","issue":"Connection string is baked into app config. Primary failover requires manual restart — during a failover event the service is fully down until an operator intervenes. This is an infrastructure gap."},{"from":"order_history_service","to":"caller","status":"risk","issue":"Any transient DB connection error (network blip, connection pool exhaustion, brief failover) immediately returns 500 with no retry. For a read-heavy service this is unnecessarily fragile."}],"contract_compliance":"Service exposes /health and Prometheus metrics, which is a good baseline. However /health only checks primary connectivity — if the primary fails over and the baked connection string is stale, /health will correctly report unhealthy but there is no automated recovery path.","data_flow_integrity":"Write path to primary is straightforward. Data flow integrity is acceptable for the write path. The lack of read replica routing is a missed optimization but does not compromise data correctness."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["No mention of logging connection pool saturation events (active connections near max 25)","No mention of logging when /health check fails — critical for correlating outages"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No connection pool utilization metric (active/idle/overflow connections) — pool exhaustion will be invisible until 500s spike","No metric for replica lag (needed once read routing is added)","No HTTP request rate/latency histogram — only DB-layer metrics exist","No metric distinguishing read vs write query performance"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No mention of distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry trace propagation) — in a microservice environment, correlating an order-history 500 back to an upstream caller will require manual log grep"]},"debugging_capability":"Moderate. Structured JSON logging with request ID and query duration is solid for single-service debugging. The gap is cross-service correlation (no tracing) and infrastructure-layer visibility (no pool metrics). An on-call engineer at 3am can find a slow query but cannot easily answer 'why are we running out of connections?'"},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Primary database failover (planned or unplanned)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"MUST-HAVE: Use a DNS-based or service-discovery-based connection string (e.g., AWS RDS endpoint, PgBouncer, or Consul) so the service automatically resolves to the new primary. Eliminate the manual restart requirement."},{"scenario":"Transient database connection error (network blip, TCP reset)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"MUST-HAVE: Add retry with exponential backoff (1-2 retries, ~200ms-1s delay) for idempotent read queries. Reads are 95% of traffic — retrying them is safe and dramatically reduces user-facing 500s."},{"scenario":"Connection pool exhaustion under load spike","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"SHOULD-HAVE: Add connection pool metrics and alert at 80% utilization. Consider increasing pool size or adding read replica routing to split load. Current max of 25 connections is thin for a read-heavy production service."},{"scenario":"Slow query / long-running transaction holds connections","handling":"partial","recommendation":"SHOULD-HAVE: Add statement_timeout at the DB session level (e.g., 5s for reads) to prevent connection pool starvation from runaway queries. db_query_duration_histogram helps detect but does not prevent."},{"scenario":"Read replica becomes available or primary overloaded","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"NICE-TO-HAVE for launch, SHOULD-HAVE post-launch: Implement read replica routing for the 95% read traffic. This is the single biggest scalability improvement available."}],"retry_config":"None. All database errors immediately surface as 500s. This is the most impactful gap for day-to-day reliability.","timeout_config":"Not specified. No evidence of query-level or connection-level timeouts. Risk of connection pool drain from hung queries.","circuit_breakers":"None. Acceptable for a single-dependency service at launch, but consider adding if read replica routing introduces multiple backends.","graceful_degradation":"None. Service is all-or-nothing — if primary is unreachable, 100% of requests fail. With read replicas, reads could degrade to slightly-stale data while writes fail, preserving ~95% of functionality."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. Straightforward and acceptable. Verify that database schema migrations (if any) are backward-compatible with the previous image.","monitoring_coverage":"Partial. DB query duration and error totals are good. Missing: connection pool utilization, HTTP-layer metrics, health check failure alerting. An operator cannot currently distinguish 'DB is slow' from 'pool is exhausted' from 'connection string is stale'.","on_call_documentation":"Not mentioned. Recommend a runbook covering: (1) what to do when primary fails over (currently: restart service), (2) how to identify pool exhaustion, (3) how to force-reconnect, (4) escalation path for DB team.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["DATABASE_URL should be dynamically resolved (DNS/service-discovery) rather than a static config value"],"new_services":["Consider PgBouncer or similar connection pooler if not already in place for failover transparency"],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Connection string resolution mechanism must change from static to dynamic","Add statement_timeout to DB session configuration","Add retry policy configuration for read queries"],"infra_provisioning":["DNS-based or service-discovery-based primary endpoint that updates on failover (e.g., RDS endpoint, Consul service, or PgBouncer routing)"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No migrations mentioned in this review scope. Order history tables are likely large and append-heavy — any future indexing changes should be done with CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Primary failover requires manual service restart due to baked-in connection string","reason":"This is a hard production-readiness blocker. A primary failover is not an edge case — it happens during maintenance windows, hardware failures, and scaling events. Requiring a manual restart means guaranteed downtime during every failover. The service needs a dynamically-resolved connection endpoint (DNS-based RDS endpoint, PgBouncer, Consul, etc.) before it is production-safe.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"Zero retry on database connection errors for a 95% read service","reason":"Transient connection errors are routine in production (network blips, pool churn, brief failover windows). With no retry, every transient error becomes a user-facing 500. For idempotent reads that constitute 95% of traffic, a simple 1-2 retry with backoff is safe and dramatically improves reliability. This is low-effort, high-impact.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Failover handling is definitively broken — baked connection string with manual restart is a clear infrastructure gap","Retry gap is clear and well-understood — read-heavy service with no retry is unnecessarily fragile","Observability baseline is decent but has specific measurable gaps"],"low_confidence_areas":["Connection pool sizing adequacy — depends on actual traffic volume, query latency distribution, and whether PgBouncer is already in the stack","Whether read replica routing is truly needed pre-launch vs post-launch — depends on expected traffic volume"],"assumptions":["PostgreSQL replicas are provisioned and available but not currently used by this service","The service runs in a containerized environment with image-based deploys","No connection pooler (PgBouncer/Pgpool) sits between the service and PostgreSQL","Traffic volume is meaningful enough that pool exhaustion under spikes is a realistic concern"]}}
